title: Cynanchum orientense
taxonomy: Cynanchum orientense belongs to the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. This classification is based on the plant's morphological and molecular characteristics. As a member of the Apocynaceae family, Cynanchum orientense shares similarities with other plants in this family, such as the presence of milky latex and paired leaves. The plant's classification is also influenced by its evolutionary relationships with other species within the genus Cynanchum. Further research on the plant's phylogeny and classification may provide more specific information about its relationships within the family Apocynaceae.
uses: Cynanchum orientense, also known as the Oriental swallowwort, has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. The plant is known to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may contribute to its potential health benefits. Some studies suggest that Cynanchum orientense may have immunomodulatory and anti-cancer properties, although further research is needed to confirm these findings. The plant is often used in traditional medicine to treat a range of conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. However, more research is necessary to fully understand the plant's medicinal uses and potential interactions with other substances.

side_effects: Cynanchum orientense side effects are not well documented, but it is known to be toxic and potentially harmful if ingested in large quantities.
